name: domain-profiles
description: Domain-specific configuration profiles for learning resource creation. Defines search strategies, special fields, terminology policies, and content structures for different academic domains: technology, history, science, arts, and general. Use when researcher or writer agents need domain-adapted behavior.
capabilities: Provides domain-adapted search strategies for technology, history, science, arts, and general topics
Defines terminology policies with bilingual glossaries (Korean and English)
Specifies content structures and document organization for each academic domain
Includes review criteria for quality assurance by AI reviewers
Standardizes persona.md sections with domain-specific guidelines
Maps search sources and quality indicators for each field
author: BitYoungjae
securitySummary: Pure documentation skill containing markdown guidelines for domain-adapted content creation. No executable code, no scripts, no network calls. Contains only Read, WebSearch, and WebFetch tool references appropriate for research documentation. The static analyzer misidentified markdown syntax (backticks, table delimiters) as code execution patterns.
valueStatement: Create adaptive educational content by configuring AI agents with domain-specific search strategies, terminology policies, and content structures. Supports technology, history, science, arts, and general topics.
limitations: Does not generate content directly - provides configuration for other agents
Does not execute code or interact with external APIs
Requires integration with researcher and writer agents to produce output
Language focus is primarily Korean and English bilingual content

useCases.title: Technology tutorials
useCases.description: Create programming guides with code examples, version notes, and best practices for Korean learners

useCases.title: Historical curricula
useCases.description: Develop history lessons with primary source citations, timeline formats, and multi-perspective analysis

useCases.title: STEM documentation
useCases.description: Build science materials with LaTeX equations, lab safety notes, and problem sets

promptTemplates.title: Basic research setup
promptTemplates.prompt: Read skills/domain-profiles/technology.md and apply the search strategy to research {topic}. List authoritative sources and key concepts.

promptTemplates.title: History content structure
promptTemplates.prompt: Read skills/domain-profiles/history.md and design a content structure for {historical_event}. Include timeline format and perspective balance guidelines.

promptTemplates.title: Science equation formatting
promptTemplates.prompt: Read skills/domain-profiles/science.md and use the equation format guidelines to explain {scientific_concept}. Include LaTeX notation and bilingual terminology.

promptTemplates.title: Arts technique guides
promptTemplates.prompt: Read skills/domain-profiles/arts.md and structure a guide for {art_technique}. Include visual reference requirements and practice exercise format.

bestPractices: Load the appropriate domain profile before any research or writing tasks
Apply terminology policies consistently - Korean term followed by English in parentheses on first use
Use domain-specific content structures to match reader expectations in each academic field
antiPatterns: Using general templates instead of domain-specific profiles for specialized topics
Skipping terminology glossaries for technical or historical concepts
Writing single-perspective content on controversial historical topics

faq.question: Which domain should I use for a topic?
faq.answer: Match the topic to its primary field: programming and APIs use technology, historical events use history, scientific concepts use science, creative disciplines use arts, and everything else uses general.

faq.question: How do I handle topics that span multiple domains?
faq.answer: Use the general language profile for cross-domain topics. Include perspectives from each relevant domain with clear section headers.

faq.question: Can I customize these profiles for my needs?
faq.answer: Yes, the profiles are guidelines. Modify terminology policies, content structures, or add domain-specific fields to match your target audience.

faq.question: How does this integrate with other dokhak agents?
faq.answer: Researcher and writer agents read domain profiles before tasks. Reviewer agents use review criteria from profiles to evaluate output quality.

faq.question: What tools can agents use with this skill?
faq.answer: Read for accessing profile files, WebSearch for finding sources, and WebFetch for retrieving web content. No file writing or code execution.

faq.question: How do I add a new domain profile?
faq.answer: Create a new markdown file in the domain-profiles directory with the same structure: search strategy, special fields, terminology policy, content structure, and review criteria.
